name: Pokémon
description: Gotta know 'em all — types, evolutions, and trainers

questions.q: Which Pokémon is #001 in the National Pokédex?

source.notes: Grass/Poison type. Evolves to Ivysaur, then Venusaur.

questions.q: What type is Pikachu, the franchise's mascot?

source.notes: #025. Evolves from Pichu (needs happiness) and into Raichu (via Thunder Stone).

questions.q: What is Charmander's Pokémon type?

source.notes: Pure Fire type. Evolves into Charmeleon then Charizard (Fire/Flying).

questions.q: What does Ivysaur evolve into?

source.notes: Ivysaur evolves at level 32 into Venusaur.

questions.q: How many Pokémon were in the original Pokémon Red and Blue games?

source.notes: Generation 1. Mew was added as a rare event Pokémon (#151).

questions.q: Who created the Pokémon franchise?

source.notes: Inspired by his childhood bug-collecting hobby. Founded Game Freak in 1989.

questions.q: In what year were the first Pokémon games (Red and Green) released in Japan?

source.notes: February 27, 1996 in Japan. Red and Blue released in North America in 1998.

questions.q: What is the name of the main protagonist in the Pokémon anime?

source.notes: Known as Satoshi in Japan. From Pallet Town in Kanto. Finally became a Pokémon Master in 2022 (26 years later!).

questions.q: In what region do the original Pokémon Red and Blue games take place?

source.notes: Based on the real Kantō region of Japan. Main cities: Pallet Town, Cerulean, Celadon.

questions.q: Who is the Pokémon professor in the original Kanto region?

source.notes: Samuel Oak. Gary Oak's grandfather. Specializes in Pokémon-human relationships.

questions.q: What are the names of the Team Rocket trio that chases Ash Ketchum in the anime?
choices: Butch, Cassidy, and Team Rocket Grunt

source.notes: Their catchphrase: 'Prepare for trouble... and make it double!'

questions.q: What is the dual type of Bulbasaur?

source.notes: Unique typing among starter Pokémon. The only Grass/Poison starter line.

questions.q: Psyduck is which Pokémon type?

source.notes: Despite the 'Psy' in its name, Psyduck is pure Water type. Misty's famous Pokémon.

questions.q: Which Pokémon is the 'genetic clone' created by scientists, serving as the main antagonist of the first movie?

source.notes: #150. A clone of Mew. Psychic-type legendary.

questions.q: What are the three original Kanto starter Pokémon?

source.notes: Grass, Fire, and Water. The pattern of 3 starter types repeats in every generation.

questions.q: What Pokémon is Ash's starter in the anime series?

source.notes: Given by Professor Oak because Ash was late. Ash refuses to put him in a Poké Ball.

questions.q: How many Eevee evolutions existed as of the end of Generation 8?

source.notes: Vaporeon, Jolteon, Flareon, Espeon, Umbreon, Leafeon, Glaceon, Sylveon.

questions.q: Pokémon Gold and Silver introduced which new region?

source.notes: Released 1999 (Japan) / 2000 (US). 100 new Pokémon. Based on Japan's Kansai region.

questions.q: Which company co-owns The Pokémon Company alongside Nintendo and Game Freak?

source.notes: Founded 1998. Creatures Inc. handles the Pokémon Trading Card Game.

questions.q: What was the Pokémon that appeared in the famous 'Pokemon Go' mobile game launch in 2016?

source.notes: Released July 2016 by Niantic. 151 original Pokémon. Became a cultural phenomenon.
